data.description:
Goal:
The existing hot/warm/cold tiering (`src/tiering.rs`) currently moves any terminal knot to cold immediately, which buries recently-terminated knots from `kno ls`. Loosen the policy so terminal knots only move to cold when BOTH (a) hot count > 110 and (b) age > 72h. On `kno ls`, if hot > 110, batch-archive oldest-eligible terminal knots until hot == 100; print one summary line. Make `kno show` transparently read cold entries via a local SQLite lookup on miss (no rehydrate, no sync â€” must stay fast). `kno rehydrate` already restores a cold knot to hot; update it to bump `updated_at = now` so the knot gets the normal 72h grace before becoming re-eligible for cold.

Reuse, do not duplicate: `cold_catalog`, `classify_knot_tier`, `rehydrate_from_events`, `cold_catalog::search`, and the existing sync flow stay. No new tombstone column. No new `kno thaw` command.

Verification:
1. Seed a test DB with 150 knots: 100 non-terminal, 50 terminal with `updated_at = now - 96h`. Run `kno ls`. Expect 100 rows; stdout contains `archived 50 knots to cold storage`; `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM cold_catalog` == 50.
2. Seed 111 knots where all terminal knots have `updated_at < 72h` ago. Run `kno ls`. Expect 111 rows; no archive summary; `cold_catalog` count unchanged.
3. Seed 150 knots (mix of SHIPPED >72h, abandoned >72h, and non-terminal). Run `kno ls`. Expect hot count == 100; the 50 oldest-eligible knots in cold_catalog; summary printed.
4. Archive a knot locally (via step 1). Run `kno sync` push+pull against a test remote. Clear local cache and rehydrate. Expect the knot still classified as cold (`cold_catalog` populated), not resurrected to hot.
5. Seed cold_catalog with 1000 entries. Time `kno show <cold-id>`. Expect output with title/state/updated_at (from catalog), wall time <25ms, no network calls, no event replay.
6. Take a cold knot. Run `kno rehydrate <id>`. Query `SELECT updated_at FROM knot_hot WHERE id=<id>`. Expect value within 1s of now, not the knot's original updated_at. Run `kno ls` and expect the knot to appear.
7. Unit-test updated `classify_knot_tier` across the matrix: (terminal, non-terminal) Ă— (age 0h, 71h, 73h) Ă— (hot full, hot not full). Terminal â†’ Cold only when age > 72h AND caller indicates hot-full condition.
8. `kno ls` on a 111-hot DB with zero eligible knots (all terminals <72h): no error, no archive, no summary.

Constraints:
- BLOCKED and DEFERRED are passive-waiting states, NOT terminal. Use the profile's `terminal_states` list (`workflow_runtime::is_terminal_state`), do not hardcode.
- No new column on `knot_hot`. No `archived_at` field. No `kno thaw` command.
- Reuse `cold_catalog` as-is (id, title, state, updated_at).
- `kno show` on cache miss: local SQLite SELECT on `cold_catalog` only. NO rehydrate, NO pull, NO sync. If caller wants full body, they run `kno rehydrate <id>` explicitly.
- Sweep runs inline on `kno ls` invocation.
- If hot > 100 but no knot is archive-eligible (e.g., all terminals <72h), accept the bloat â€” no error, no spam.
- Summary line to stdout; per-knot archive details to file log only.
- `kno rehydrate` must bump `updated_at = now` when materializing back to hot; existing behavior may not do this.
- Parent/child invariant (parent cannot terminate until children terminal) is already enforced upstream.
